description_en: The site of Dainichiji temple classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A wooden five-ring stupa (gorintō) classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of crafts. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A bronze temple gong (waniguchi) classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of crafts. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A pair of wrought-iron candlesticks classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of crafts. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A mizunara oak tree classified as a monument (natural monument). It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A collection of documents classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A Buddha's footprint stone classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of crafts. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A memorandum document concerning rice harvesting, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A painted image of the White-Robed Kannon classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of painting. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A painted image of the Monju (Manjushri) bodhisattva classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of painting. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A painting depicting Daruma (Bodhidharma) in his youth, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of painting. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

name_en: Painting of the Thirty-Three Kannon of the Western Provinces
description_en: A painted image of the Thirty-Three Kannon of the western provinces pilgrimage, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of painting. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

name_romaji: Moto Dainichiji Mamorifuda Efuda no Hangi Tsuketari Munafuda
name_en: Printing Blocks for Amulets and Charms of the Former Dainichiji, with Ridge Tablet
description_en: Printing woodblocks for amulets and picture-charms of the former Dainichiji temple, with an accompanying ridge tablet, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of crafts. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A votive picture tablet (ema) depicting gold panning, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of historical materials. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A vermilion-seal charter (goshuinjō) classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of historical materials. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

name_romaji: Kan'ei Shiraiwa-ryō Sōdō Mazawa-mura Sanrei Kuyōhi
name_en: Memorial Monument for Three Spirits of Mazawa Village in the Kan'ei Shiraiwa Domain Uprising
description_en: A memorial monument for three spirits connected to the Kan'ei-era Shiraiwa domain uprising, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of memorial monuments. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A temple gate plaque of Hondōji inscribed "Yudonosan," classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of wooden plaques. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
The main hall of Kanayama Shrine classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of structures. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

name_en: Statues of the Kongō Rikishi Guardians
description_en: Statues of the Kongō Rikishi guardian kings classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of sculpture. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A seated statue of Kōbō Daishi classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of sculpture. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A stone monument dedicated to Yudonosan classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of stone objects. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

name_en: Proxy-Pilgrimage Tower Group of the Former Dainichiji
description_en: A group of proxy-pilgrimage stone towers of the former Dainichiji temple, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of stone objects. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

name_en: Proxy-Pilgrimage Tower Group of the Former Hondōji
description_en: A group of proxy-pilgrimage stone towers of the former Hondōji temple, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of stone objects. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

name_en: Painting of the Ascent of Mount Gassan
description_en: A painting depicting the pilgrimage ascent of Mount Gassan classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of painting. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

name_romaji: Ita-e Dewa Sanzan Onna Kōchū Sankei-zu
name_en: Panel Painting of a Women's Pilgrimage Group to the Three Mountains of Dewa
description_en: A wooden panel painting depicting a women's pilgrimage group to the Three Mountains of Dewa, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of painting. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A kagura sacred dance classified as an intangible cultural property. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

name_en: Stone Pavement of the Rokujūrigoe Highway
description_en: A stone pavement of the Rokujūrigoe highway classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

name_en: Site of the Anchūbō Superintendent's Residence
description_en: The site of the Anchūbō superintendent's residence classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

name_en: Pictorial Land Map of Yoshikawa Village
description_en: A pictorial land map of Yoshikawa village classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of historical materials. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
The graves of Daihachi and Daishichi classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A gong-beating alms-solicitation ritual of Hondōji classified as an intangible cultural property. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A temple gate hanging plaque of Hondōji inscribed "Yudonosan," classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of wooden plaques. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A hanging plaque of the Hondōji founder's hall inscribed "Kaisandō," classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of wooden plaques. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

name_en: Memorial Tower for the Parents of Tenyū
description_en: A memorial tower for the parents of Tenyū classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of stone objects. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.
A votive picture tablet (ema) depicting log rafting, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of votive tablets. It is town-designated in Nishikawa Town.

A three-bay, single-entrance temple gate of Ryōgoji classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of structures. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1962-11-27, and located in Nobezawa.

name_en: Paper Votive Tablet (Brushed by Kanō Yoichi)
description_en: A paper votive tablet (ema) brushed by Kanō Yoichi, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of painting. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1962-11-27, and located in Nakamachi (on deposit at the Bashō and Seifū Museum of History).

A pictorial map of Obanazawa village classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of painting. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1995-01-31, and located in Nakamachi (on deposit at the Bashō and Seifū Museum of History).

name_romaji: Nyoirin Kannon Kakebotoke Den Miroku Bosatsu Zazō Kakebotoke
name_en: Hanging Buddha of Nyoirin Kannon and Hanging Buddha of the Reputed Seated Miroku Bodhisattva
description_en: Hanging Buddha plaques (kakebotoke) of Nyoirin Kannon and of the reputed seated Miroku bodhisattva, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of sculpture. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1962-11-27, and located in Nogurosawa.

name_romaji: Shō Kannon Bosatsu Ryūzō (Mokuchō Ippongi Zukuri)
name_en: Standing Statue of Shō Kannon Bodhisattva (Single-Block Wood Carving)
description_en: A single-block wood-carved standing statue of the Shō Kannon bodhisattva classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of sculpture. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1969-12-20, and located in Niu.
